title: His Recantation.
content:
Love, I recant,
    And pardon crave
    That lately I offended;
    But 'twas,
    Alas!
    To make a brave,
    But no disdain intended.

    No more I'll vaunt,
    For now I see
    Thou only hast the power
    To find
    And bind
    A heart that's free,
    And slave it in an hour.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "His Recantation.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
